items.title: Bundle preprocessing with the estimator
items.avoid: Fitting imputer, encoder, and scaler in separate notebook cells then saving only the final classifier.
items.prefer: One Pipeline or Pipeline([ColumnTransformer(...), model]) fit on X_train; persist the whole object with joblib.
items.why: Training and serving share one ordered transform chain; CV and hyperparameter search see realistic preprocessing.
items.example:
```python
pipe = Pipeline([('prep', preprocess), ('clf', LogisticRegression())])
pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
joblib.dump(pipe, 'v1.joblib')
```

items.title: Split before any learnable transform
items.avoid: Computing medians, modes, or scaler stats on concatenated train+validation+test frames.
items.prefer: train_test_split (or CV) first, then pipeline.fit on the training partition only.
items.why: Preprocessing parameters must not observe holdout distributions—see data leakage for the general pattern.
items.example: Use `cross_val_score(pipeline, X, y, cv=5)` instead of scaling all of X manually before CV.

items.title: Encode step order in the pipeline graph
items.avoid: Arbitrary step order such as scale → impute or encode after the estimator.
items.prefer: Numeric branch: impute → scale. Categorical branch: impute → encode. Estimator last.
items.why: Downstream transformers assume finite, correctly typed inputs from upstream steps.

items.title: Make train and serve paths identical
items.avoid: Manual pandas cleaning in training that the production API skips.
items.prefer: Wrap shared hygiene in FunctionTransformer or a custom transformer inside the saved Pipeline.
items.why: Train/serve skew is a silent accuracy killer when encoders see different string variants or dtypes.
items.example: FunctionTransformer(strip_upper, validate=False) as the first pipeline step for object columns.
